design_philosophy.core_loop: Arcade-style aerial combat: fly, identify enemy patterns, execute evasive maneuvers and counterattacks, progress through mission stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing aggressive offensive positioning against incoming enemy fire and environmental hazards in constrained airspace
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a high-performance fighter jet in intense one-on-one and multi-enemy dogfights with skill-based precision flying
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Sequel deliberately iterates on first game with mechanical additions (plane rolling capability) and hybrid mode structure (campaign + dedicated 1v1 dogfight mode), suggesting designers valued both structured progression and competitive matchplay

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden invulnerability and stage select cheats (accessible via Game Genie codes) suggest developers anticipated demand for difficulty adjustment, but base game appears to offer no official scaling

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential mission stages unlocked through completion; dogfight mode available as separate competitive mode with computer or two-player opponents
replayability_hooks: One-on-one dogfight mode against computer opponents (including named rival Stalin Fortimesky in Round 6)
Multiple named enemy pilots suggesting varied matchup challenges

world_building_techniques: Enemy pilot character names and portraiture (e.g., Stalin Fortimesky) establish rival identity
Mission structure implies ongoing conflict arc across campaign
Visual character differentiation in portraits suggests personality-driven adversaries

boss_design.philosophy: Named rival pilots serve as signature encounters within competitive dogfight structure; suggests boss fights framed as prestigious matchups rather than mechanical gauntlets

faction_indicators: Player vs. named rival pilot framework suggests patriotic/military service identity
danger_signals: Russian antagonist naming (Stalin Fortimesky) reflects Cold War-era geopolitical framing common to 1989-1990 military action games
environmental_storytelling: Portrait-based pilot characterization conveys personality without explicit dialogue
